C[pathogenix: Always hard to know what's biggest when you've got all the corners :)
Shakti: the general idea is: urgent moves first, big moves second
C[Shakti: black often fixes the shape here first, not allowing white to play there
pathogenix: hmmm.... that's troublesome. Would you have played that immediately if this were even, or would you have kept it for later?
C[Shakti: I think it is urgent, so normally it should be played right away
Shakti: so if we follow the same basic principle, urgent before big moves, black may want to fix his shape locally again, now with S8 or similar
C[Shakti: but, once black has q13, he cannot let w get q14 also and get perfect, strong solid shape
C[Shakti: question: would connecting at T10 now be big or urgent?
Shakti: urgent involves usually, a group getting weak or strong
Shakti: weak usually means poor eyespace, little room to manouver
pathogenix: Not sure, if white continues to poke at black's group there, black can connect to the corner, so maybe it's miai and not so urgent
Shakti: agreed, so we can probably say that this is a big move
C[pathogenix: Black's group looks settled-ish... is another move here urgent?
Shakti: this is a life and death question, it's urgent if w can kill or make ko in corner
pathogenix: I think I'll play safe
Shakti: yes, that is a good idea
Shakti: since if w kills this, it's almost game over
C[Shakti: we had the urgent vs big move idea, now we can introduce another
pathogenix: I hoped you'd respond at O5
pathogenix: and if you didn't, I could attach there and try to get some shape
pathogenix: But I don't think it will work
Shakti: thickness is a difficult concept, but in the obvious cases, like very big walls and such, we usually wanto avoid ploaying near them as much as possible
Shakti: so j5 was pretty good direction, away from the wall
C[pathogenix: this looks very bad for black :/
C[Shakti: with m5 black lost some pace, and w got to trap black near the wall
C[pathogenix: Yes, I should have jumped toward the centre instead
pathogenix: Life is hard for the poor kyu player :)
pathogenix: I considerd M10, but it looks like it will just make the running battle worse
Shakti: when in deep trouble, one can ask, can i sacrifice this group?
Shakti: so let's look at that first
Shakti: if black dies here, can he win?
pathogenix: Only if you give me the rest of the board
Shakti: so: black must either run out, or make eyespace
pathogenix: running is difficult here because I'm running alongside a wall
pathogenix: and you get even more influence by attack me as I go
pathogenix: but there isn't much eye space
pathogenix: Maybe M10 is correct, because it helps to do both
C[Shakti: this is what we call a overplay
Shakti: a move that tries something that really isn't possible in the situation
Shakti: and we can see some options
C[Shakti: the o4 stones have some bad aji (bad potential in a possible cut)
Shakti: when in troubel, use oponents weaknesses
pathogenix: Yes, that was the idea behind M5
pathogenix: It's my first of the game
pathogenix: I have to go change nappies and things
pathogenix: I think getting out is most important still
C[pathogenix: The aji can wait a moment
pathogenix: White N12 is too painful
C[Shakti: you are doing very well

pathogenix: I have to go and make lunch, I'm enjoying the game though, especially not that I'm not dying in a corner somewhere :D
C[pathogenix: Cut first think later. Part of me wants to reduce the size of your group by attacking with F12 and K10, but I think that's greedy. Better to keep E13 weak and try to take the corner

C[Shakti: the direction of play is plausible, however i am not sure of the shape since getting w to push in like this is similart to having a one-space jump where w peeps and black ignores, usually one would not ignore such a peep
Shakti: with that being said, w not very thick so no certain that the fight will work out for w in the end
C[pathogenix: not sure if this is good or awful
pathogenix: Actually, I think maybe it works...
pathogenix: okay... whatever I try in the centre is going to come to nothing if you break through at this point and engulf the corner, so make shape first

Shakti: usually that's not good for b

Shakti: so it may be be enough

C[pathogenix: I had an epiphany last night. I've had a losing streak these last few weeks, I thought maybe I was making bad shape, maybe my reading was too weak, I think I've seen in this game that I cause too many complex situations, and then don't look after them.
C[Shakti: some play like that, Ilja Shiksin for example
Shakti: but it requires tremendous reading skill
pathogenix: I don't want to play that way, I want to play honte, calm moves but I panic
Shakti: the key is to put trust in thickness and learn how it works, but it's too early to dig too deep into that i think
Shakti: direction of play, basic reading, and some groundlevel stuff will get one far
pathogenix: Probably. I know I ought to use thickness to attack, but I can never find the way. I keep meaning to read Attack and Defense, but work interferes :)
Shakti: thickness is a interesting thing, it may help you to get 2 points extra in the endgame, rather than killing something big
C[Shakti: so, it's very hard to use correctly
C[Shakti: when one is beginner, things like basic reading takes priority, because failing to read a life and death position may cost 50 pts,
pathogenix: interesting... I think blocking to the right is bigger, but if I block from below, I have a large wall to attack your group with
Shakti: right, the left group has more aji, so making the wall towards that area makes sense
